text: The surgeon evaluates the tumor, noting its narrow stalk along the lingual tonsils. A cut is made through the lingual tonsils at the base of the tongue to debulk the lesion, revealing a narrow and superficial invasive front. The majority of the lesion is removed, exposing the lingual tonsillar tissue.

The surgeon makes an incision through the lingual tonsil along the glossotonsillar sulcus on the right side, extending anteriorly and inferiorly towards the vallecula, and over to the left side. A deeper resection is performed over the invasive portion of the tumor to achieve a deep negative margin. The final cuts are made through the vallecula with clear visualization of the epiglottis, removing the invasive base of the cancer in block.

The specimen from the base of tongue resection is placed on the back table and inked for pathology. Frozens are taken to ensure negative margins. The post-resection view of the base of tongue shows a significant change from the preoperative state, with fibrin sealant applied to cover the exposed raw surfaces.
